terms.definition: A named storage location with a type and a value.
The compiled form of Java source code that the JVM runs.
Explicitly converting a value to another compatible type.
The remainder left after integer division, written with %.
Putting one control structure inside another.
&& or || stopping as soon as the result is already known.
The numeric position of an item in an array or list.
Arrays whose same indexes describe related information.
The <> in new ArrayList<>() that lets Java infer the generic type.
A variable in a method header that receives a value.
A value or expression supplied when calling a method.
Using the same method name with different parameter lists.
A blueprint that defines fields and methods.
An instance created from a class.
A variable that belongs to an object or class.
A no-argument constructor; Java can supply one only when none is declared.
A constructor that receives another object of the same type and copies its state.
A record constructor that accepts one value for each record component.
One parent or interface reference using behavior from different object types.
A subclass supplying its own version of an inherited method.
A method with no body that a concrete subclass must implement.
A contract listing behavior a class must provide.
The keyword used when a class agrees to an interface contract.
A Java interface for keyboard pressed, released, and typed events.
Putting a value into a variable using =.
Two references pointing to the same object, tested with ==.
A method that returns a String representation of an object.
An object representing a problem during program execution.
An exception from using a null reference as if it were an object.
A try form that closes a resource such as Scanner automatically.
One shared field that belongs to the class.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: A method that subclasses cannot override.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: Unused objects kept reachable by references, preventing cleanup.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection

terms.definition: The object that provides drawing methods in Swing.
A request for Swing to redraw a component later.
A position, usually x then y, in the drawing area.
A compact declaration for an immutable-style data carrier.
A method that returns a record component, such as name().
An object mainly used to hold related data.
Choosing an overridden instance method based on the actual object at runtime.
A method changing outside state, printing, or doing I/O.
An unfinished method kept in place while a program is being built.
A reserved Java word such as class, public, or return.
Statements enclosed by braces { }.
Joining Strings with +.
The first line of a method that includes access, return type, name, and parameters.
terms.examFocus: Final: be able to write and read method headers without mixing up return type and parameters.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: putting the return type inside the parameter list or forgetting it completely.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: name the parts of a Java method header.

terms.definition: The type of value a method promises to send back, such as int, String, or void.
terms.examFocus: Final: decide whether a method should return a value or just print/do an action.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: printing a value when the method was supposed to return it.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does void mean as a return type?

terms.definition: A return type meaning the method does not return a value.
terms.examFocus: Final: use void for actions like printing or changing object state.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: trying to use the result of a void method in an expression.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: give one example of a void method.

terms.definition: A smaller method used by another method to keep code organized.
terms.examFocus: Final/build focus: break a larger task into smaller testable pieces.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: writing all logic in main instead of using helper methods.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why are helper methods useful?

terms.definition: A keyword such as public, private, or protected that controls visibility.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: know why fields are often private and methods are often public.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: making every field public and losing encapsulation.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: which access modifier protects a field from outside classes?

terms.definition: Keeping object data protected and exposing controlled behavior through methods.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: connect private fields with getters, setters, and object responsibility.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: thinking encapsulation only means hiding code, not controlling access to state.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why should fields often be private?

terms.definition: A method that returns the value of a field.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: recognize simple accessor methods in class code and UML.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: changing the field inside a getter.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does a getter usually return?

terms.definition: A method that updates the value of a field, usually with validation if needed.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: know when a setter changes object state and when validation matters.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: shadowing the field with a parameter and forgetting this.field.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does a setter usually do?

terms.definition: A reference to the current object inside an instance method or constructor.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: use this to separate fields from parameters and call overloaded constructors.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: writing name = name; instead of this.name = name;.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why do constructors often use this?

terms.definition: Creating more than one constructor with different parameter lists.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: recognize multiple ways to initialize the same class.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: giving overloaded constructors the same parameter list.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: how can two constructors have the same name?

terms.definition: A keyword used to refer to the superclass, often to call a superclass constructor or method.
terms.examFocus: Final: connect super() to constructor chaining in inheritance.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: forgetting that super(...) must be first in a constructor when used.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does super() call?

terms.definition: An inheritance relationship where a subclass is a specialized kind of superclass.
terms.examFocus: Final: use the is-a test to decide whether inheritance makes sense.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: using inheritance for has-a relationships that should use fields instead.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: is a Dog an Animal, or does a Dog have an Animal?

terms.definition: Java choosing the overridden instance method based on the actual object type at runtime.
terms.examFocus: Final tracing: predict which overridden method runs through a parent reference.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: choosing the method only from the reference type instead of the actual object.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what matters more for overridden methods, reference type or actual object type?

terms.definition: An operator that tests whether an object is an instance of a class or interface.
terms.examFocus: Final: recognize safe type checks before casting objects.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: using instanceof instead of good polymorphic design when overriding would be cleaner.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what question does instanceof answer?

terms.definition: A class that cannot be directly instantiated and may contain abstract methods.
terms.examFocus: Final: know the difference between abstract class, concrete class, and interface.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: trying to create an object directly from an abstract class.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: can you write new AbstractClass()?

terms.definition: A class that can be instantiated because it implements all required abstract methods.
terms.examFocus: Final: know when a subclass becomes usable as an object.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: leaving an abstract method unimplemented but expecting the class to compile.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what must a concrete subclass do with abstract methods?

terms.definition: A KeyListener method called when a keyboard key is pressed down.
terms.examFocus: Final/KeyListener: connect keyboard events to movement and repaint logic.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: updating position but forgetting repaint().
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: which KeyListener method reacts when the key goes down?

terms.definition: A KeyListener method called when a keyboard key is released.
terms.examFocus: Final/KeyListener: know the three required methods even if some are empty.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: not implementing all methods required by the interface.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why can a KeyListener method body be empty?

terms.definition: A method used to compare meaningful object equality instead of just reference equality.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 2/final: decide when to use == and when to use equals().
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: using == to compare two different String objects by content.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: when should you use equals() instead of ==?

terms.definition: A reference variable that points to no object.
terms.examFocus: Final: trace code carefully before calling methods on a reference.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: checking that a variable exists but not that it refers to an object.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what happens if you call a method on null?

terms.definition: A structure for handling exceptions so the program can respond to errors.
terms.examFocus: Final: understand which code goes in try and what catch handles.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: catching an exception but not fixing or reporting the problem.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what is the purpose of catch?

terms.definition: An exception that can happen when Java cannot find or open a requested file.
terms.examFocus: Final/files: recognize why file reading code often needs exception handling.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: assuming the file path is always correct.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why can file reading fail even if the code is written correctly?

terms.definition: A method that belongs to the class and can be called without creating an object.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection
terms.examFocus: Final: distinguish class-level behavior from object-level behavior.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: trying to directly use instance fields inside a static method.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why is main static?

terms.definition: A field where each object has its own separate copy.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection
terms.examFocus: Final: compare instance fields with static fields when tracing object state.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: thinking all objects share every field.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: does an instance field belong to the class or each object?

terms.definition: Java automatically reclaiming memory from objects that are no longer reachable.
terms.topic: Static, Final, Memory, and Garbage Collection
terms.examFocus: Final: understand reachability and why references matter.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: thinking garbage collection means variables disappear immediately.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what makes an object eligible for garbage collection?

terms.definition: A Swing method where custom drawing code is placed for a component.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 1/final: know the correct signature and why drawing belongs here.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: calling paintComponent directly instead of calling repaint().
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: where should Swing drawing code usually go?

terms.definition: A Graphics method that draws text at an x, y coordinate.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 1/final: recognize common Graphics drawing methods and coordinate arguments.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: forgetting that y grows downward in many drawing coordinate systems.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does drawString need besides the text?

terms.definition: A Graphics method that draws a filled rectangle using x, y, width, and height.
terms.examFocus: Midterm 1/final: trace coordinates and dimensions in drawing code.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: mixing up x/y position with width/height size.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what are the four main numbers for fillRect?

terms.definition: A value listed in a record header that becomes part of the record state.
terms.examFocus: Final: know how record components create accessors and stored values.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: expecting record accessors to use getName() instead of name().
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: how do you access a record component called name?

terms.definition: An object designed so its stored data is not normally changed after creation.
terms.examFocus: Final: connect records to data carrier objects and limited mutation.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: assuming a record means every referenced object inside it is deeply immutable.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: why are records often called immutable-style?

terms.definition: A diagram showing classes, fields, methods, and relationships.
terms.examFocus: Final: read attributes, behaviors, visibility, and inheritance arrows.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: confusing a class diagram with an object diagram or flowchart.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what does a UML class box usually contain?

terms.definition: Overloading changes the parameter list; overriding replaces inherited behavior in a subclass.
terms.examFocus: Final: compare these two terms because exams often place them side by side.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: saying overriding happens in the same class with different parameters.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: which one needs inheritance?

terms.definition: A field belongs to an object or class; a local variable exists only inside a method or block.
terms.examFocus: Final: trace scope and lifetime when the same name appears in two places.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: thinking a local variable can be used anywhere in the class.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: where does a local variable live?

terms.definition: Returning sends a value back to the caller; printing only displays output.
terms.examFocus: Final/coding: choose return when another method needs the value.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: passing tests by printing when the method should return.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: can printed output be used in an expression?

terms.definition: Two or more references pointing to the same object.
terms.examFocus: Final tracing: know when changing one reference changes the shared object seen by another reference.
terms.commonTrap: Common trap: assuming assignment copies the object instead of the reference.
terms.checkQuestion: Before flipping: what happens after b = a for object references?
